pitch: After the AI apocalypse, guard a fragile VIP across the ruins and get them to the last working radio tower before the signal dies.

pitch: A roguelite tank shooter where every run pushes deeper through procedural floors of upgrades and armored bosses.
inspiration: Battle City

pitch: A browser-based zombie survival FPS inspired by Call of Duty Zombies — fight endless waves, buy weapons, unlock perks, and survive as long as you can.
inspiration:
Call of Duty Zombies was my escape — late nights, dark corridors, surviving one more round with friends. That world was peace to me.

I rebuilt it by talking to an AI. Not writing code — describing memories. Every sound, every mechanic, every fix: one message at a time through AutoPilot by AutoGPT. What once took a studio, I built alone in a conversation.

This is proof that anyone's idea can become real. The undead are waiting.

pitch: Command a high speed cargo ship through dangerous waters, dodge escalating threats, and deliver critical supplies before global trade collapses.
inspiration: I was fascinated by how a single disrupted shipping route can affect fuel prices, supply chains, and everyday people worldwide. Strait Run turns that global fragility into an arcade survival challenge.

inspiration: Literally this tweet last week from three.js (https://x.com/threejs/status/2045024314400051552); combined with a lifelong obsession with maps, mapgames, boats, maritime law, and shipwrecks.

inspiration: I love old school shmups, and I love roguelikes like Hades and Slay the Spire. This is the ultimate combo!

pitch:
Small footprint, big release
Light design, deep experience
A national 3D runner game
inspiration: I made this game as a hobby, wanting to create something simple and stress-relieving.

pitch: Navigate a sky-high straddle carrier through a live multiplayer container port where every lift is a bet—stack the honest work, chase triple pay at the back fence, and don’t get caught moving the wrong box under the cop’s nose.
inspiration: Machine operators on instagram

pitch: High-performance marketing, one flap at a time.
inspiration: Flappy Bird

pitch: A lightning-fast western duel where you must shoot before your opponent in a split-second reaction test.
inspiration: I’m really interested in making games and exploring how far AI tools can help in creating something fun and engaging. This jam felt like the perfect opportunity to experiment and see how much I could build with AI assistance.

pitch: A fast, chaotic multiplayer survival showdown where you roll, dodge, and outlast your rivals on collapsing tiles until someone takes the plunge.
inspiration: Indie 2D Game Maker games from the early 2000s.

pitch: Break through the chaos and free the cat before it’s too late.

pitch: A high-pressure strategy game where you race against time, exhaustion, paperwork, and bad luck to secure a future abroad before everything falls apart.
inspiration: I made this game to capture the pressure of trying to start over in another country. Instead of fighting monsters or chasing fantasy quests, the player is dealing with things that feel much closer to real life: burnout, paperwork, unreliable recruiters, rising costs, and the constant feeling that time is running out. I wanted every week in the game to feel like a meaningful tradeoff, so the tension comes from trying to hold yourself together long enough to create a future somewhere else.

pitch: A fast-paced movement shooter built around parkour, firefights, and titan-scale combat.
inspiration: I kept coming back to the idea of a Titanfall inspired game because nothing else I brainstormed felt as exciting, or right for the competition. I didn't know what AI was capable of vibe coding at the start of this competition either, so this felt like a really ambitious project to attempt.

pitch: Simple controls, action-packed, easy to learn hard to master
inspiration: Tibia, Vampire Survivors, Risk of Rain, and Slay The Spire
engine: no idea bro it was vibe coded :D

pitch: An action shooter game in which it’s all about parrying and blasting away.
inspiration: Parry mechanisms for dark souls mainly inspired this game

pitch: World of Warships - but with missiles
inspiration: flypieter: "now I have multiplayer"

pitch: Tekken-style 1v1 brawler in your browser - six fighters, signature supers, KO cams, zero loading.
inspiration: Grew up on Tekken but every new 3D fighter today is 50GB and gated behind logins. Wanted to prove you can ship the feel of a Tekken match char select, combos, KO cam, signature supers in a browser tab that loads before you blink. AI-coded end-to-end with Claude in a single night.

pitch: CRUMBLE is a couch-competitive castle wrecking game where you use your phone to bring your friend’s kingdom crashing down.

pitch:
You’re a vibe coder inside a retro simulation.
No buttons, no mercy just a terminal and your brain.

Type the right command, chain your actions, and let it run.
Every move is a prompt. Every mistake keeps looping.

Find the perfect sequence… or stay stuck in the level forever.
inspiration: The game was inspired by the current AI hype, and we combined it with retro gameplay, kind of blending the past with the present.

pitch: 1st-Person Vacuum Racing Game
inspiration: I've seen all this awesome games on Levelsio X that I decided to give it a go. Its my first vibe coded game ever. The idea came looking at my robot cleaner in my room and I thought this could be fun. Like how can I be faster and more efficient.

pitch: Slide ancient hieroglyph stones across a sacred 5×5 grid, decipher the sequence carved in the shrine above, and unlock the wisdom the ancients buried for those worthy enough to find it.
inspiration:
I wanted to build something that felt like it had weight to it not just mechanically, but emotionally. The sliding puzzle mechanic is simple, but I layered it inside an Egyptian temple world because I was drawn to how the ancient Egyptians thought about the soul the idea that what you carry, what you put down, and what survives says everything about who you are.
The treasure you unlock at the end isn't gold or points. It's five chapters of text that speak directly to the player about resilience, identity, and what you're capable of. I wanted the game to feel like it was built for the person playing it.
The hieroglyphs, the stone, the music, the torchlight all of it was in service of one question: what if a puzzle game made you feel something real when you finished it?

pitch: Collect shards so the kid alien can go back home
inspiration: Wanted to make something different and challenging

pitch: A cool guy on an electric scooter weaving through traffic, collecting coins, and hopping portals to other games.
inspiration: I wanted to show my brother what is possible today with the help of AI.

game_name: FlyBy - Dude, your fly is open!

pitch: A 2.5D survivor game that turns sustainability quizzes into power-ups while you battle garbage monsters.
inspiration: As a father of four daughters, I wanted to build something that’s not only fun, but also has a positive impact on them. Sustainability is such an important topic, and I believe the earlier you learn it, the more you can shape the future, so I turned that idea into a game.

pitch: Choose one of 8 mechs, level up, equip gear, and buy near-infinite upgrades to battle against the enemy Void Armada galactic invasion.
inspiration: This game jam! :)

pitch: Sprint through 5 parallel worlds at breakneck speed, dodging obstacles that vanish before your eyes, leaping through reality-warping portals — until a golden JAM EXIT pulls you into the multiverse.
inspiration: Subway Surfers and Temple Run for the endless runner core, combined with the idea of parallel realities — what if every portal didn't just change the scenery but actually transported you to a different dimension with its own rules, colors, and feel? The disappearing obstacles came from wanting to add a psychological twist: you can't fully trust what you see, which makes every run feel unpredictable even when you know the patterns.

pitch: Your webcam turns your arms into a sword. Slash through storybook worlds to take back the fairy tales the Story-Eater stole.

pitch: A cyberpunk arcade flight game where you hijack a robo-taxi, race through a neon city to earn your freedom, and survive rival AI taxis, storms, and other obstacles along the way.
inspiration: When I was younger, my friend and I would play "Simpsons: Hit and Run" often. I thought it'd be fun to build a similar taxi-simulation game, but with a cyberpunk setting. I also wanted to see what it would be like to use other AI technologies like Suno and ElevenLabs for making music and spoken dialog to create a cinematic gaming experience.

inspiration: I love driving on the open road so I created a platformer that simulates the experience while keeping the controls limited to three buttons!

pitch: Fight your way from bare fists to explosive RPGs through a dying cyber-city in a single-file Three.js arena shooter — no install, no loading, instant play.

pitch: Multiplayer dress up runway game built with my 9yr old daughter.
inspiration: After messing about with my kids making a Harry Potter broom stick game they found really funny as the broomsticks went up the characters bum

pitch: Destroy 5 towers, evade attacking mobs, solve the maze.
inspiration: I wanted something that felt fast and stylish like normal air combat games, but with a tighter, and a puzzlng map, so your flight is with purpose. I’ve always been a fan of mazes, so I decided to build a large scale maze and add action to it.

pitch: Grow a living 3D neural network around a luminous core — every upgrade reshapes the mesh, spawns workers along new paths, and pulses energy back into a growing singularity.
inspiration: I wanted to make an idle game where the thing you're idling isn't numbers on a screen but a living geometry — a network that visibly grows, pulses, and reshapes itself as you invest. The visual starting point was this CodePen (https://codepen.io/sabosugi/full/YPGLJpv); from there it grew into a quiet, meditative idle game about watching a tree of light form in real time.

pitch: A spooky theme park builder where you design, build, and manage attractions while keeping visitors entertained in a fun, eerie atmosphere 👻🎢
inspiration: My game is inspired by the classic 90s PC game Theme Park World. It’s a game that was part of my childhood, and nowadays it’s practically impossible to play. For that reason, I decided to create my own version of the game to help bring attention to this great genre.

pitch: Ship Snatch is a browser-based, top-down hex tower defense and base builder with a claymation pirate vibe - you grow an archipelago hex by hex, place and upgrade defenses, and hold your Captain’s Keep against waves of enemy ships and sea monsters, solo or in co-op for up to 32 players.
inspiration: I wanted to create a cute claymation style game that will appeal to my 9yo nephew but still be interesting to play by adults as well.

pitch: A high speed survival shooter runner where a hostile AI sabotages your escape and breaks down as you outplay it.
inspiration: I wanted to push beyond typical AI generated games into something where the AI itself becomes the antagonist. Inspired by how language models shift from confidence to uncertainty under pressure, I designed a system where the AI reacts to player skill in real time, evolving from mockery to panic to desperation within a single run.
made_with: I have used bolt.new, then cursor, then claude code, then windsurf, antigravity and codex. then i used gpt for image generation for logo, and suno for music. I also tried tripo 3d, but it was paid, was trying to go for scrappy build

pitch: A real-time multiplayer .io game where you grow from a brown dwarf into a primordial black hole, devouring rivals under real astrophysics — inverse-square gravity, slingshots, Hawking radiation, and spaghettification included.
inspiration: I love .io games and I love astrophysics — this is what happens when you mash them together. Every mechanic (gravity, slingshots, tidal stripping, black-hole collapse) is based on actual physics instead of scripted gameplay.

game_name: Dr. Frogsworth and the Stolen Formulas
pitch: A retro pixel-art platformer (Mario × Mega Man homage) where Dr. Frogsworth blasts and hops through 5 realms of internet decay to liberate the meme formulas from the patent-hoarding Wojak King and Dr. Croak to keep the internet funny and free.
inspiration: Growing up on games like Super Mario Bros. and Mega Man 2, and watching modern meme culture get eaten by corporate patent-hoarding and algorithmic slop. I wanted a retro side-scroller where the villains are the ones trying to *prevent* memes from being freely stolen and remixed — because in classic meme culture, stealing = sharing = evolution. Dr. Frogsworth is the hero who made the formulas to be spread forever; the Wojak King and Dr. Croak are trying to gate keep them. It's a love letter to NES-era platformers and the early, free internet.

pitch: Paddle into massive waves, carve across the breaking face, and collect stars before the foam swallows you.
inspiration: I made it for my workmates who have never surfed to feel a small part of the joy I feel when surfing - not about tricks, but the simple fun physics of riding a wave.

pitch: Collect API Credits to complete tasks and quests as you venture in a world where terminals are king.
inspiration: Loved Zelda growing up and wanted a vibe / api / adventuregame

pitch: Degenerate Cookie Clicker with Recycling Bottles and Gambling
inspiration: I wanted a game to play during homeoffice hours on the side, an idle game that I can always check in and that keeps me entertained during boring tasks or learning sessions :)

pitch: A riot has broken out at Martian base, and the Dark AI is invading and taking over the energy center. Riot Bots Players can choose to fight independently or form alliances to defend their colony. What will become of this Solar Intelligent Age? An open-ended ending awaits!
inspiration: A friend sent me Vibejam's games, makes me want to try 3D game dev. I like developing hardware and love battlebots, so I want to try making robot battle game.

pitch: The Operator is a simulation game inspired by “Papers, Please,” where the player takes on the role of a mainframe operator managing an LLM server in a retro cassette-futurism setting
inspiration: The core inspiration is definitely "Papers, Please." I wanted to take that feeling of bureaucratic pressure and 'checking documents' but transpose it into a 1980s mainframe office, where instead of passports, you’re managing the chaotic output of a dusty, analog AI server.

pitch: 0에 가장 가깝게 멈춰라. 넘으면 끝. 단일 버튼으로 즐기는 3D 웹 타이밍 서바이벌
inspiration: fly.pieter.com의 1주일 바이럴 + Flappy Bird의 즉결 긴장감 + "0을 넘지 않는다"는 결단의 메타포

inspiration: The challenge of strict limitations. Vibe coding only, ASCII graphics, <5MB in size, procedural audio (except AI voice-over), no other assets, while still creating something atmospheric that tells a story

pitch: never end
inspiration: slime

pitch: Make waves, steal ducks, own your zone — a splashy 4-player pool party where mastering the water's wake is the key to victory.
inspiration: I wanted to make something as simple and instantly playable as Mario Party , but exciting, nerve-wracking, and replayable enough that you'd keep going "one more round!" And I wanted to do it all in the browser with Threejs.

pitch: Upgrade your weapons, unleash absolute chaos, and smash through endless hordes of rogue Clawbots in this fast-paced neon roguelite shooter.
inspiration: I love rogue likes. The biggest inspiration was the old browser game Boxhead.

pitch: Test your speed and reflexes in an endless, fast-paced match-3 puzzle game where you continuously find and pop sets of three identical tiles to beat the line and rack up your highest score.
inspiration: I saw an ad for a similar game and loved the concept, so I decided to build my own version from scratch to make it completely free for everyone to play.

pitch: You and a bunch of strangers ram cars in a dusty arena, just dumb fun

pitch: Blizzard Brawl is a fast-paced browser snow duel where you build defenses, deploy gadgets, and outplay rivals in a shrinking frozen arena.
inspiration: `I saw an article about how levelsio made money from a game he built in just three hours.`

pitch: Outlast 7 AI opponents on a crumbling neon arena where every collision cracks the floor beneath you.
inspiration: A combination of mario kart and fall guys
engine: My private custom game engine built on top of ThreeJS

pitch: Vibe Sentinel is a 10-level turn-based dungeon roguelike where you fight escalating shadow beasts across 10 visually distinct biomes — with endless mode, badges, and a portal that drops you straight into the next Vibe Jam game.
inspiration: Classic grid-based roguelikes like the original Rogue and early Nethack — the idea that every move matters and tension comes from resource management, not reflexes. I wanted to bring a visual identity that changes every level — from a forest grove to a volcanic maze to a void sanctum.

pitch: Your go-to browser arcade for quick, fun breaks.
inspiration: The community. The passion of the community and getting 150 users trying it out.

pitch: A cinematic morality runner where every life you take returns in Purgatory, and you must face the consequences.
inspiration:
I was inspired by last year's winning game The Great Taxi Assignment by 
@TomasBencko, and wanted to push the concept further. Transforming a familiar mechanic into a more provocative experience about mortality, judgement, and consequence.

pitch: Asymmetric tower defense, where players can play as defense or attackers against other players, bots, and push for high scores on the leaderboards
inspiration: Balloon Tower Defense + Chess

pitch: Dive into the abyss.Harvest the souls of the damned and survive in this retro 3D Dungeon Crawler.
inspiration: Retro-style dungeon crawlers

pitch: A Love Letter to 80s Dungeon Crawlers
inspiration: Old school dungeon crawlers, Dungeons and Dragons, Dark fantasy.

pitch: A fast and chaotic web-first 3D critter brawler where cute animal fighters smash rivals off the arena.
inspiration: I've always enjoyed party games for having fun with friends and enjoying some healthy, fun competition. I like all kinds of video games, but mostly I see them as a way to escape and have fun, plain and simple.

pitch: You're a vibe coder trapped inside your own buggy game, fighting enemies while the code keeps corrupting itself wave by wave.
inspiration: I kept shipping AI coded projects that had weird glitches I couldn't always tell from features. Code Rage is a game about that exact vibe, you're stuck inside a codebase that corrupts itself wave by wave and has to decide what's worth fixing.

pitch: A hidden-role multiplayer game where LLM agents race to stabilize (or secretly corrupt) a cascading prompt graph before the cluster collapses.
inspiration: I wanted to see if I could gamify the AI development experience. I took the Among Us loop and reskinned it with LLM-specific tasks and themes, replacing the traditional traitor with a Hallucinator role to see how social deduction shifts when the 'sabotage' is themed around AI logic. The codebase is fresh and the logic is 99% there; now it just needs the first contact with players.

pitch: An MMO shooter where matches are block rounds and players fight to secure Bitcoin payouts
inspiration: My love for quake, tribes and bitcoin!

pitch: Multiplayer Zombie Building Survival Game with increasing difficulty
inspiration: I like zombie survival games such as DayZ and I though tit would be cool if that would work online in the browser!

pitch: A fast-paced MMOTPS on ThreeJS
inspiration: Fast movement shooter games like Tribes, Destiny, Titanfall... and space theme games like Star Citizen, Starfield, No Man's Sky,...

pitch: Survive in an eternal void and pet the penguin forever, watch your fingers!
inspiration: A game I created on Roblox years ago that I eventually want to make into a VR title, this is the general idea of the gameplay and aesthetic as a web-playable demo, there is still a lot of work left to do on optimization

pitch: Save your hospitalized dad's 40-year Akihabara tech shop with rapid-fire Street Fighter combo shifts or the bank wins.
inspiration: I was building DogSprite, my own AI pixel art MCP. The day my sprite generator actually worked, I saw Pieter announce the Vibe Jam. Perfect timing to test the full stack end-to-end: Claude Code wrote 99% of the game, my MCP generated every sprite, and I designed it to feel like a casino machine  fast rounds, loud combos, global stakes pulling you back for one more run. 100% vibe coded, start to finish.

pitch: Intense Sci-Fi Space Battle
inspiration:
Ive already build many things with AI, but never a video game.

pitch: Relentless waves of enemies—from slow-drifting asteroid clusters that shatter into deadly fragments, agile hunter drones force players to adapt quickly as each new threat brings unique movement patterns, attack styles, and escalating danger, All made on a local LLM.

engine: 1 Page, HTML-5 JavaScript Canvas, Probably. How the hell do I know I'm just a vibe coder lol.
made_with: qwen3.5-35b-a3b-gguf GGUF · UD-Q4_K_XL: LM Studio + Open Code, LM Studio, Unsloth Studio, LM Studio + Open Claw.

pitch: Smack down 50 of the worst bosses imaginable in brutal card battles — solo or drag a friend into the nightmare.

pitch: Abstract arcade runner where a crystal drifts through infinite procedural space — hold to SHATTER through obstacles, release to RECOMBINE and collect energy. 5 biomes, boss waves, power-ups, global leaderboard, 981KB instant load.
inspiration: I wanted to prove that a single AI tool (Claude Code) could build a polished, production-quality arcade game from scratch — one-button mechanic with deep systems: 22+ game systems, custom PostFX shaders, procedural audio with convolution reverb, 5 biomes with AI-generated skyboxes, and a global leaderboard. The core design was inspired by the tension between risk and reward in games like Ikaruga and Super Hexagon.

pitch: Pawn Market is Polymarket for chess. Trade live game positions, buy and sell at changing odds, profit before the result, and even earn from your own games.
inspiration: I hate losing chess games, and I love the fantasy of inside trading on Polymarket,  so I combined them

inspiration: I loved the old Lunar Lander–style cabinets and wanted a tight, browser‑friendly version I could tweak and share with friends.

pitch: Simple browser-based 1v1 real-time multiplayer card game where you push the frontline to destroy the enemy base to win

My goal is to make a game that connects random players that can play the game on any device, fully multiplatform but the same battle field. If the there's nobody to match I will generate an AI Bot player can fight + tournaments with real prizes in the future after the Jam.

This is a game brings me childhood nostalgia, where I played on dirt tracks, digging holes, making ramps with rocks. 
Think of clues to the Hot Wheels miniatures, but we did it all with our hands or a small shovel, earth, stones, caps from soda bottles. It was fun for hours.

pitch: paint to fight!
inspiration: my son

pitch: In 20 blind rounds, strategically allocate your workforce across the AI supply chain, decode hidden market signals, and outsmart 5 cunning AI rivals to dominate the industry.
inspiration:
Over the past few years, I've seen numerous reports about the artificial intelligence industry—opportunities and risks coexist, time is of the essence, companies make high-risk decisions with incomplete information, everyone is vying for scarce computing resources and data, and there's a constant tension between short-term gains and long-term consequences.

This is the inspiration behind my game. I wanted to transform this chaotic, stressful, yet absurdly captivating reality into a compact and easy-to-play gaming experience—a 20-round blind-play synchronous strategy game where players must allocate manpower throughout the AI ​​supply chain, interpret hidden market signals through tensions and news, cope with the shortage of computing resources, and strive for ultimate victory.

This theme feels particularly relevant today, and I hope it not only prompts reflection on the world we've collectively built but also provides entertainment in a fun and relaxing way.

pitch: Have fun solving the endless 3D Maze!
inspiration: Solving maze is always fun!

pitch: Drive a photorealistic 3D news van around YOUR real city, chase breaking stories, and out-scoop rival stations in 2 minutes of live-broadcast chaos.

pitch: Astropilot VR is a VR racing game in space, for your phone.
inspiration: I wondered if phones had JS browser APIs that could be used to make an immersive VR-like experience. As it turns out, they do! And #vibejam was the perfect opportunity to build a proof of concept. It turned out better than I could have imagined, and I'm obsessed with it now. Can't wait to add more features.

pitch: APU is a mythic voxel mountain-climbing survival game where you leave a warm Andean town, brave cold, wind, and sheer cliffs, and fight to reach the summit alive.
inspiration: I was inspired by three things I care about: game development, mountain climbing, and my roots in Peru. I wanted to make a game that brought all of those together, while also experimenting with vibe coding to see how far these new tools have come in helping creators turn ideas into reality.

pitch: Collect flowers and return safely to the hive, without being killed by angry wasps!
inspiration: I actually started thinking something involving the Fibonacci sequence because it's cool, which lead me to sunflowers, and then bees. I wanted something engaging and original, so I thought bees vs wasps would be cool. Above all, something that's intuitive and that you can jump into right away, and that will keep you playing. As you go up levels stuff gets added, you get honeycomb, extra lives, and then more lethal wasps come in (Tarantula Hawk and Executioner at level 12+15), which was inspired by Brave Wilderness getting stung by them on YouTube. The Wasps actually spawn in Fibonacci initially, but then it gets to much, so I had to limit it.

pitch: Prisdil+ is an interactive behavioural strategy game inspired by the Iterated Prisoner's Dilemma — one of the most studied and fascinating concepts in game theory, economics, and psychology where either cooperate or defect across games.

pitch: An isometric survival game where you gather, craft, cook, and fight to stay alive in a procedural world full of hostile creatures, harsh weather, and long dark nights.
inspiration:
I've always loved games, but now, as a husband and father, I unfortunately don't have time to play anymore. However, Pieter's idea and the evolution of AI provide me with enjoyment, allowing me to create games for this game jam while continuing my work.

I really like isometric perspective games; I played Diablo 2 for over 10 years. Don't Starve was one of the last games I played before my daughter was born, and I really enjoyed it, so I thought it would be a good idea to make a version of the game with Vibe Code.

pitch: A tense, high-altitude survival game where you must balance on a tightrope suspended above a neon-lit cyberpunk city while fighting against increasingly violent storms and unpredictable winds.
inspiration: I was inspired by the nail-biting tension of real-life high-wire walkers and wanted to translate that intense feeling of vertigo and precarious balance into a digital experience. I wanted to take a very simple mechanic, keeping a pole centered and elevate it by surrounding the player with a moody, procedural cyberpunk cityscape and dynamic weather systems, making every single step forward feel like a fight for survival. Also, one thing is that i also like extremely challenging and frustrating games, which made think about this game concept.

pitch: Strangers across the internet hitting the same boss. When it dies, it comes back stronger.

I loved the idea of taking a peaceful flooded neighbourhood and turning it into a chaotic playground where the only way to settle a property dispute is to blast your neighbor into the waters, patrolled by the neighbourhood's escaped pet goldfish that mutated into 'Gutter Sharks' after the drains clogged with leaf mulch.

pitch: An old-school text-based MMORPG where your character keeps progressing even offline, with rare loot and a fully player-driven economy—all playable for free in your browser.
inspiration: Frustration with idle/MMO games becoming paywalled, bloated, or dying pushed me to build a simple, long-term “check-in” game that respects your time and keeps progressing even when you’re offline.

pitch: A COD Zombies/Megabonk inspired Rougelite, zombie survival shooter.
inspiration:
I have always been interested in game design and game creation overall. I've been wanting to make games for a while but attempting to use big engines like Unity or Unreal always scared me off, I found them too hard to even attempt at learning. Since AI came around and Cursor blessed us with their presence I have found that using agentic tools is actually the way forward for me personally. 

Since I started using Cursor, I have attempted several different game projects with 0 initial knowledge on the underlying technologies beforehand and Z-Bonk for me is the product of all those hours I spent learning those systems.

pitch: a darkly comedic clicker where you erase coffee, continents, and eventually the sun itself to find out how many clicks it takes to destroy civilization.
inspiration: I was a bit inspired by the style of Plague Inc., although the concept is different, and also by Cookie Clicker.

pitch: Monopoly meets indie hacking. Acquire products. Dominate niches. Bankrupt your friends' startups.
inspiration: When I first heard about a game competition run by indiehackers, the immediate thing that came to my mind was Monopoly. Indiehacking culture can have striking similarities with some aspects of Monopoly, and that prompted me to create an Indie hacking themed monopoly.
engine: No engine, it's a board game

pitch: Grab a gun, shoot others and last stand wins.
inspiration:
Hello! I'm a UEFN & Unity developer with 6 years of experience. I know these engines well, but I'd never built a Three.js game before. I saw the game jam and thought, it's a good opportunity for creating project on ThreeJS.
The game itself is actually based on one of my UEFN maps. I had a lot of fun playing it in Fortnite, so I figured it'd be fun for others too, and it felt like a solid fit for a minimal jam project.
1.5 days for vibe-coding the game, server, and bug fixes, then 1.5 days for polish and deployment. 3 days total for a fully finished game. Wild man!!.
Honestly I don't think I could have shipped something like this in Unity in 3 days, especially with multiplayer. Three.js is amazing and also SUPER OPTIMIZED for PHONES! even for my 6 years old Galaxy Note10Lite.
Thanks for putting this jam together. It really pushed me to try something new.

pitch: A rubber duck terrorizes a city with a honk button, evolving from "Lil' Quacker" to gun-wielding "Duck Norris" as chaos escalates — GTA meets Untitled Goose Game, 100% coded by AI

inspiration: The real deer in Nara, Japan actually bow back when tourists bow to them. The game turns this charming real world interaction into a tense stealth puzzle where every bow is a 1.5 second window of vulnerability.

pitch: Planets in orbit meet golf for all with some chaos
inspiration:
I'm a big fan of games that you can play with friends to wind down after a long competitive gaming session. You just hop on in call and go play. That's what inspired me, creating a game that lets you breath and get some laughs in.

Then I thought about things that I enjoy and space was one of them, then meteorites came to my head.They fly and they crash into planets, which made me think that the a meteorite could be a golf ball. After that, it was just a spiral of ideas to make a Cosmic Golf game.

pitch: KartBlitz is a vibrant 3D arcade racer that combines precision drifting with stylized, high-octane worlds for an instant-play browser experience.
inspiration: KartBlitz was inspired by a love for classic arcade racers and the challenge of building high-performance 3D experiences directly in the browser. Drawing from the nostalgia of 90s karting games and the collaborative vision of the Vibe Jam 2026 'Webring,' I wanted to create a sense of speed and style that players could jump into instantly, connecting them to a wider world of creators through the portal system.

pitch: an idle guild management game featuring vivid warriors
inspiration: Ore ni Hatarakette Iwaretemo

pitch: First-person wave shooter where you defend humanity from alien creatures pouring through a dimensional rift in deep space
inspiration: Wanted to have a fun time doing something completely different.

pitch: A horde survival game where your weapon runs on momentum — keep moving or your guns go quiet.
inspiration: I wanted to make a horde survival game where movement is the weapon system. Instead of using movement only for positioning, I built the core mechanic around momentum: keep moving and stay lethal, slow down and lose pressure. The goal was to make a fast, instantly playable browser game that feels familiar at first, but quickly reveals a more aggressive twist.

pitch: Open Source agentic engine for text-based games
inspiration: I was inspired by the challenge. Can I make an engine? I have to try it.
made_with: OpenCode, Windsurf, Cline (vscode extension), Codex app, Jules

pitch: Rocket VTOL stunt flight simulator with realistic physics, snappy action, and time trial.
inspiration: I played the game on fly.pieter.com and thought "what if I tipped the thrust axis up" and this is the result.

pitch: Vibe your way through the universe!
inspiration: The tweet from Levelsio

pitch: You need to find the way out of the maze.

pitch: Multiplayer 3D combat arena where you fight players and 3 bosses simultaneously while racing to 10,000 points first
inspiration: Death Race film, classic arcade combat games, and the chaos of browser-based multiplayer IO games.

pitch: Blocky Pursuit - Evade cops in a blocky city — don't get busted.
inspiration: I have been playing Smashy Road for a long time. Now create my own version in a sleepless night.

pitch: A multiplayer game of taxation, survival, and dragon fire
inspiration: I've had this idea sitting on my disk since 2009 - never had time and means to do it

pitch: Asymmetric multiplayer dogfight — drones kamikaze an aircraft carrier while F-16s defend it, over the open ocean.
inspiration: I watched the news and thought 'this would make a great game' — turns out asymmetric drone warfare is more fun when nobody actually gets hurt and the explosions have particle effects.

pitch: A zombie-survival game with augments. Will you get the perfect Exodia combination and get the highest score?
inspiration: The gameplay is inspired by a Flash game I used to play a lot as a kid, called Boxheads. And I added a little twist because I like games like TFT and Hades with a ranomized augment system.

pitch: Portal meets Halo meets Counterstrike; Nostalgic Memories
inspiration: The word "portal" on levelsio tweet made me instantly think about the game portal. He also mentioned he wanted to see multiplayer games so I decided to combine games from my childhood (Portal, Halo, Counterstrike, Cod, Unreal Tournament etc.)

pitch: Multiplayer party mini-games, your phone is the controller
inspiration: Playing family games, my school coding contest

pitch:
COLOSSEUM BETS.
⚔️ Procedural Gladiators
🥬 Tactical Cabbage Throwing
📜 Comedic Combat Logs
💰 Shared Leaderboard (Become a Patrician!)

pitch: Less luck then poker , the wannabe most lucrative card game ever
inspiration: The amount of badbeats pro players have in poker.

pitch: Survive the endless road in a scavenger’s Lada — tow wrecks for scrap, trade up your engine and tires, and outrun empty fuel, dead batteries, and the wasteland weather.
inspiration: My favourite 2 games: Hard Truck Apocalypse and Drive Beyond Horizons.

pitch: Firework Frenzy is a one-button arcade fireworks game about timing, not aiming.
inspiration: I created this game for mobile years ago and i wanted to re-do it so it was closer to what I thought it could be.
engine: JavaScript with an HTML5 <canvas> and DOM/CSS UI.

pitch: Survive the chaos of the ZOO as a tactical Red Panda, evolving your "BambuShots" in a cozy-but-brutal rogue-lite world
inspiration: I was inspired by brotato, that is a simple RogueLite game, but I wanted to make the most cozy rogue lite possible

pitch: An endless runner where the player needs to rush avoiding to get wet
inspiration: ASked for game suggestion to my wife and the experiment started

pitch: A 100% vibe-coded WW1 tower defense running on a C game engine that was made from scratch, where every sprite, explosion, and sound effect is synthesized at runtime from pure code — no art files, no audio files, just 430KB of WebAssembly.
inspiration: I'm a big fan of older tower defense games such as custom games from Warcraft 3, like Line Tower Defense (LTD) or Element TD. I wanted to challenge Claude Opus 4.6 to create a game engine from scratch, with a minimal footprint in game size and render times.
engine: Custom game engine built by Claude Opus 4.6 from scratch.

pitch: conjure stuff and defend it
inspiration: to try realtime text-to-3D in a multiplayer shooter
made_with: 70% GPT-5.4 in VS Code, 30% Claude Code in The Oasis

pitch: 3d open world
Neon cyberpunk scooter races in your browser, procedural tracks, boost and coins, up to 10 players per room, no install.
inspiration: We wanted a fast, social browser game that feels like a night ride through a neon city—Mario Kart’s energy and camera feel, but with scooters and a cyberpunk vibe. The jam pushed us toward something instantly playable, multiplayer-first, and built with AI-assisted iteration so we could focus on feel: boost, ramps, and silly competition without installs or accounts.

pitch: Aquarium - survival game for humans and agents
inspiration: Believe that we all are in aquarium, and this game is a simplified simulation of our world :)

pitch: Personality game
inspiration: Jung

pitch: Online PVP retro boxing!
inspiration: Old school Atari Boxing

pitch: Bumper King is a fast-paced arcade bumper car battle where you smash rivals, grab powerups, dodge hazards, and fight to wear the crown.
inspiration: I wanted to make a fast, chaotic arcade game that feels instantly fun, inspired by bumper cars and the growth-and-domination loop of Snake.io.
made_with: Codex Tool using GPT-5.4 and ElevenLabs

pitch: A vertical space shooter rendered entirely in ASCII text on HTML5 Canvas, inspired by Scramble (1981)
inspiration:
As a kid, I used to spend hours playing my uncle's TomyTronic Scramble — a handheld console game from 1981 that was ahead of its time. Just a tiny VFD screen and a game that kept pulling you back for one more run.
I wanted to build something inspired by that experience. Not a remake, but a game built around the same idea — that you don't need fancy assets or a game engine to make something enjoyable. You need the right mechanics.

pitch: Race as cute capybaras and cats in a Mario Kart-inspired 3D browser kart racer with drifting, items, and AI opponents.
inspiration: I like capybaras!

pitch: AI tower defense where you defend your data center with GPU turrets and send tokens to overwhelm your opponent — 1v1 multiplayer with Sam Altman voice callouts
inspiration: BT Defense meets the AI compute war  what if GPU clusters and data centers were the battlefield

pitch: The ultimate movie quiz
inspiration: Being blown away that AI actually can reliably source great questions for the specific quiz format that I have implemented. I was already toying with the idea in my mind, and the contest pushed me over the edge to actually build it.
engine: Thymeleaf templates on top of Spring Web MVC

pitch: Sail the open seas, battle rival pirates with cannon fire, and explore islands for treasure in this multiplayer top-down naval combat game.
inspiration: I played Sid Meier's Pirates (old Xbox 360 game) when I was a kid and wanted to make a similar game today.

pitch: A fast, strategic 2-player card battle on a 3×5 grid where micro-wins compound into momentum and every move shifts the game.
inspiration: I wanted to create a game that captures the intensity of competitive strategy in short bursts, where every move matters and players feel constant momentum shifts instead of long, drawn-out gameplay.

pitch: A seamless browser MMORPG where players explore a mystical voxel world with instant multiplayer connectivity.
inspiration: I wanted to create a fast-paced MMO that combines Minecraft-style exploration with classic shooter mechanics. Built entirely in one morning with AI to prove that complex multiplayer genres are now accessible to everyone.

pitch: Defend your oil tanker from pirates with guns and grenade launchers while steering your ship to safety.
inspiration: I've never made a game before, so this sounded like a fun little challenge

pitch: metaverse with optional web3 wagering support
genre: Action, Adventure, metvaerse, has many games. parkour, open world, 7 minigames, slots... it has 100k lines of code. please check my https://whitepaper.waddle.bet

pitch: FPS Sandbox Game
inspiration: i wanted to try threejs and spacetimedb

pitch: A daily symbol-decoding puzzle where each symbol is a consistent instruction — learn the language, crack the phrase.
inspiration: I wanted to build something genuinely original, a threeway between a word puzzles, cryptic crosswords and maths notation. This is their illegitimate baby. The mechanics came first, then the game grew around them.

pitch: Orbit a star, slingshot through gravity wells, and fire curved photon beams to destroy waves of enemies in this 3D orbital combat arena.
inspiration: The physics of orbital mechanics and gravitational lensing — I wanted to create a game where gravity isn't just a background force but the core mechanic that shapes every shot, every dodge, and every slingshot maneuver.

pitch: A simple cars game with no bs
inspiration: This game was made by my cousin and he wanted to learn to code and I said lets start with a car game and he got excited

pitch: Call Of Duty RPG game
inspiration: Secondwar was the first PHP game I build 20+ years ago

pitch: Game Dev Story meets MADtv
inspiration: I've lived it. XD
engine: I really really really don't know. I'd guess ThreeJS?

pitch: A fast-paced 3D pong battle where you smash balls, break barriers, and outlast your opponent in real-time online matches.
inspiration: Pong was the first game I have ever played and I always asked how it would be to not play it with only one enemy but a whole group of people :D

pitch: Tower defence with cats
inspiration: Wanting to try @karpathy's auto research to build something

pitch: Train your multitasking skills, become the best AI Agents manager

pitch: The TBPN boys got their bag, now it's your turn!

pitch: green light red light squid game style

pitch: A free card game for indie hackers. Play Paid Ads. Survive DDoS Attacks. Dodge Hacker News Roasts - the real vibe without the investment.
inspiration: I've been an indie hacker for almost 10 years now, and I always wanted to bring the feeling to friends and family - now they can, without actually spending thousands of hours and money into it!

pitch: Silly browser game
inspiration: Say the word on beat challenges

pitch: Experience a modern jet fighter aerial combat simulation where players pilot their aircraft, utilizing machine guns, missiles, and flares to engage in intense dogfights with AI-controlled enemy planes in single-player missions.
inspiration: A sudden whim about the international situation

pitch: Defend the skies! Intercept incoming missiles and protect the nation in this action-packed challenge
inspiration: I wanted to help children and families in Israel cope with the stress of sirens and war. By turning the 'Iron Dome' defense into an interactive experience, we give people a sense of control and resilience while they are in the safe room

pitch: A free-to-play tactical card game with 4,276 unique cards, real-time ranked multiplayer, and 6-tier AI —  built in < 15 days by one developer using AI as a dev partner.
inspiration: Not entering the competition — the game predates the jam period and requires login, just finished the game and launched yesterday & have login so I can send packs to people that try the Beta for free. Just hoping to get eyes and feedback from industry leaders in AI gaming. Built a full production multiplayer card game in 30 days as a solo dev using AI. 4,276 cards, ranked matchmaking, 6-tier bot AI, 16 clans, server-authoritative architecture. Would love any feedback from the judges, thank you.
engine: React, TypeScript, Websockets, No Game Framework.

pitch: Navigate a pitch-black forest with only a flickering lantern in this atmospheric first-person horror game where shadowy figures vanish
inspiration: Classic atmospheric horror games and the tension of navigating darkness with limited visibility. I wanted to create that "something's out there" feeling.

pitch: Slay the spire meets Spore
inspiration: Slay the spire mechanics and people on X making creatures randomly generated with Three.js
made_with: Antigravity with Gemini 3.1, then I switched to codex.
